entities.name: John Dee
entities.description: An occultist whose encryption techniques in Monas Hieroglyphica are compared to Joyce's linguistic methods.

entities.name: Monas Hieroglyphica
entities.description: A work by John Dee containing encryption techniques and symbolic structures.

entities.name: Giordano Bruno
entities.description: Philosopher who proposed that the geometrical figure is the visible number and humans can become cooperators of operating nature through numbers.

entities.name: the four elements
entities.description: Fundamental components of the material world, linked to directions, humours, and stages of the alchemical work.

entities.name: the four humours
entities.description: Biological fluids linked to elements and directions.

entities.name: the four stages of the hermetic Work
the four winds
entities.description: Winds associated with directions, elements, and humours.

entities.name: angel princes
entities.description: Angels placed above the four winds.

entities.name: ouroboros
entities.description: The tail-biter, prototype of endless rotation.

summary: The passage explores the intersection of literary, alchemical, and geometric symbolism in works like Joyce's Finnegans Wake and the philosophies of Giordano Bruno. It details complex systems of correspondences between elements, winds, humours, and angelic hierarchies.
